DISCOVERY 612, FURTHER RESPONSES TO REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS, SET TWO, IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. PLAINTIFF'S OBJECTIONS ARE OVERRULED AND ORDERED STRICKEN. AS PLAINTIFF HAS POSSESSION OF THE REQUESTED DOCUMENTS, THEY MUST BE PRODUCED. AS PLAINTIFF HAS RESPONDED THAT HE HAS PRODUCED ALL OF THOSE DOCUMENTS IN HIS POSSESSION, HE MUST IDENTIFY THEM WITH PARTICULARITY. PLAINTIFF IS NOT REQUIRED TO MAKE A REQUEST FOR THE DOCUMENTS FROM HIS HEALTH PROVIDERS. PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS DENIED. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. MATTER NOT REPORTED.

DISCOVERY 612, DEFENDANTS UNUM LIFE'S AND UNUM GROUP'S MOTION TO COMPEL COMPLIANCE WITH SUBPOENA IS DENIED. THE MOTION WAS NOT TIMELY BROUGHT. THE COURT IN UNZIPPED APPAREL, LLC V BADER (2007) 156 CAL.APP.4TH 123 HAS HELD THAT A RESPONSE TO A SUBPOENA CONSISTING ONLY OF OBJECTIONS, WITHOUT THE PRODUCTION OF ANY DOCUMENTS, IS THE RECORD WHICH BEGINS THE 60 DAY PERIOD WITHIN WHICH A MOTION MUST BE BROUGHT. PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS DENIED. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. MATTER NOT REPORTED.

THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: Defendants Unum Life's And Unum Group's Motion To Compel Compliance With Subpoena is denied. The motion was not timely brought. The court in Unzipped Apparel, LLC v Bader (2007) 156 Cal.App.4th 123 has held that a response to a subpoena consisting only of objections, without the production of any documents, is the record which begins the 60 day period within which a motion must be brought. Plaintiff's request for sanctions is denied. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. MATTER NOT REPORTED. (EAH/612)

Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents, Set Two
Off Calendar Dec-03-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
CATHERINE T.S. GREGORY, ESQ., (415)433-0990, FOR DEFENDANT/MOVING PARTY, UNUM LIFE INSURANCE COMPANY OF AMERICA

THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: Further Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents, Set Two, is granted in part and denied in part. Plaintiff's objections are overruled and ordered stricken. As Plaintiff has possession of the requested documents, they must be produced. As Plaintiff has responded that he has produced all of those documents in his possession, he must identify them with particularity. Plaintiff is not required to make a request for the documents from his health providers. Plaintiff's request for sanctions is denied. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. MATTER NOT REPORTED. (EAH/612)
